heading: Standardizing commercial inspections
label: 87k Standardizing commercial inspections
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (c), was in the original “this Act” and was translated as reading “this part”, meaning part B of act , known as the United States Grain Standards Act, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Aug. 11, 1916

children.text: identify inspection instruments requiring standardization under subsection (b);
establish performance criteria for commercial grain inspection instruments;
develop a national program to approve grain inspection instruments for commercial inspection; and
develop standard reference materials or other means necessary for calibration or testing of approved instruments.

children.heading: General inspection procedures
children.label: b General inspection procedures
children.text: To ensure that producers are treated uniformly in delivering grain, the Secretary shall develop practical and cost-effective procedures for conducting commercial inspections of grain with respect to the application of quality factors, that result in premiums and discounts. The procedures shall be made available to country elevators and others making first-point-of-delivery inspections.

children.heading: Inspection services and information
children.label: c Inspection services and information
children.text: To encourage the use of equipment and procedures developed in accordance with subsections (a) and (b), the Secretary shall provide for official inspection services by the Secretary, States, and official inspection agencies and provide information on the proper use of sampling and inspection equipment, application of the grain standards, and availability of official inspection services, including appeals under this chapter.

children.heading: Standardized aflatoxin equipment and procedures
children.label: d Standardized aflatoxin equipment and procedures

children.text: establish uniform standards for testing equipment; and
establish uniform testing procedures and sampling techniques;
